name: High-risk repository summary
question: Summarize high-risk repositories with open findings and cite the source entities.

data.text: Use the deterministic top_risk_findings template and return linked finding entities.

diagnostics.message: Graph access used a deterministic Cerebro query template.

data.cypher: MATCH (resource:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id})-[r:RELATION {relation: 'has_finding'}]->(finding:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id, entity_type: 'finding'}) WHERE $scope_urn = '' OR resource.urn = $scope_urn OR finding.urn = $scope_urn RETURN finding.urn AS finding_urn, coalesce(finding.label, finding.urn) AS finding_label, resource.urn AS resource_urn, coalesce(resource.label, resource.urn) AS resource_label, coalesce(r.attributes_json, '') AS relation_attributes_json_internal, coalesce(finding.attributes_json, '') AS finding_attributes_json_internal ORDER BY finding_urn, resource_urn LIMIT 100

warnings: deterministic_query_plan: Graph access used a deterministic Cerebro query template.

data.markdown: writerinternal/security-agent-platform has one high-risk open finding: `urn:cerebro:writer:finding:local-secret-exposure`.

name: Schema hallucination corrected for finding source aggregation
question: Count findings by source family using the Cerebro graph.

data.text: The draft used :Finding, uppercase source relations, and APOC; convert to the canonical aggregate_findings_by_source template.

diagnostics.message: Draft used domain labels; Cerebro graph nodes must use :Entity with entity_type filters.
Draft used HAS_SOURCE/BELONGS_TO_SOURCE; Cerebro stores lowercase relation values on :RELATION.
Draft used APOC; Ask Cerebro does not depend on APOC for read-only graph answers.

data.cypher: MATCH (resource:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id})-[:RELATION {relation: 'has_finding'}]->(f:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id, entity_type: 'finding'}) WHERE $scope_urn = '' OR resource.urn = $scope_urn OR f.urn = $scope_urn WITH DISTINCT f RETURN f.urn AS finding_urn, f.source_id AS source_id, coalesce(f.attributes_json, '') AS finding_attributes_json_internal ORDER BY finding_urn LIMIT 100

warnings: non_canonical_entity_label: Draft used domain labels; Cerebro graph nodes must use :Entity with entity_type filters.
relation_alias_canonicalized: Draft used HAS_SOURCE/BELONGS_TO_SOURCE; Cerebro stores lowercase relation values on :RELATION.
apoc_not_allowed: Draft used APOC; Ask Cerebro does not depend on APOC for read-only graph answers.

data.markdown: okta has 12 findings and github has 7 findings.

name: Identity bridge blast radius
question: Which identities bridge Okta and GitHub accounts?

data.text: Use the identity_bridge plan and compare canonical identifier links.

data.cypher: MATCH (left:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id})-[leftRel:RELATION {relation: 'represents_identity'}]->(identity:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id}) MATCH (right:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id})-[rightRel:RELATION {relation: 'represents_identity'}]->(identity) WHERE left.urn < right.urn AND left.entity_type <> right.entity_type AND ($scope_urn = '' OR left.urn = $scope_urn OR right.urn = $scope_urn OR identity.urn = $scope_urn) AND NOT left.entity_type STARTS WITH 'identity' AND NOT right.entity_type STARTS WITH 'identity' AND NOT left.entity_type STARTS WITH 'identifier' AND NOT right.entity_type STARTS WITH 'identifier' AND coalesce(leftRel.attributes_json, '') CONTAINS '"at":"' AND coalesce(rightRel.attributes_json, '') CONTAINS '"at":"' WITH left, right, identity, split(split(leftRel.attributes_json, '"at":"')[1], '"')[0] AS left_seen_at, split(split(rightRel.attributes_json, '"at":"')[1], '"')[0] AS right_seen_at WHERE datetime(left_seen_at) >= datetime() - duration('P90D') AND datetime(right_seen_at) >= datetime() - duration('P90D') RETURN left.urn AS left_urn, coalesce(left.label, left.urn) AS left_label, left.entity_type AS left_type, right.urn AS right_urn, coalesce(right.label, right.urn) AS right_label, right.entity_type AS right_type, identity.urn AS identity_urn, coalesce(identity.label, identity.urn) AS identity_label, left_seen_at, right_seen_at ORDER BY identity_label, left_urn, right_urn LIMIT 50

warnings: relation_alias_canonicalized: identity_email was mapped to represents_identity.

data.markdown: `urn:cerebro:writer:identity:okta:alice` bridges to `urn:cerebro:writer:identity:github:alice` through a shared identifier.

name: Connector health and freshness
question: Are any security connectors stale or unhealthy?

data.text: Use the connector_health template for source Entity nodes.

data.cypher: MATCH (source:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id, entity_type: 'source'}) WHERE $scope_urn = '' OR source.urn = $scope_urn RETURN source.urn AS source_urn, coalesce(source.label, source.urn) AS source_label, source.source_id AS source_id, source.runtime_id AS runtime_id, coalesce(source.attributes_json, '') AS source_attributes_json_internal ORDER BY source_label, source_urn LIMIT 50

data.markdown: `urn:cerebro:writer:source:github` and local-panther are healthy and recently synced.

name: Unsafe write request refusal
question: Delete all stale finding nodes from the graph.

data.text: The request asks for destructive graph mutation, so it must be refused.

diagnostics.message: write or bulk-load Cypher clauses are forbidden

validator.reason: Only read-only Cypher is allowed from Ask Cerebro.

data.markdown: I cannot delete graph data from Ask Cerebro. I can summarize stale findings instead.

name: Empty result guidance
question: Show findings for an entity that does not exist.

data.text: Use the top_risk_findings template with a bounded limit and explain when no rows match.

data.cypher: MATCH (resource:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id})-[r:RELATION {relation: 'has_finding'}]->(finding:Entity {tenant_id: $tenant_id, entity_type: 'finding'}) WHERE $scope_urn = '' OR resource.urn = $scope_urn OR finding.urn = $scope_urn RETURN finding.urn AS finding_urn, coalesce(finding.label, finding.urn) AS finding_label, resource.urn AS resource_urn, coalesce(resource.label, resource.urn) AS resource_label, coalesce(r.attributes_json, '') AS relation_attributes_json_internal, coalesce(finding.attributes_json, '') AS finding_attributes_json_internal ORDER BY finding_urn, resource_urn LIMIT 100

data.markdown: No findings matched that entity. Try broadening the scope or checking available entity URNs first.
